_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse the organisation of ideas in paragraphs and extended texts, and to evaluate the impact of that organisation on meaning.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse the organisation of ideas in paragraphs and extended texts, and to evaluate the impact of that organisation on meaning.
y: How ideas are organised is inseparable from what they mean — structure shapes meaning.
Evaluating organisational impact develops our ability to create well-structured texts of our own.
This skill is essential for producing and assessing the quality of extended academic writing.
eg: We analyse how a text's paragraph structure builds an argument step by step.
We evaluate whether the order of ideas in a text creates the most effective impact.
We discuss how different organisational approaches — deductive versus inductive, for instance — affect how meaning is received.
We compare two texts on the same topic with different organisations and evaluate which is more effective.
We reorganise a text and evaluate how the changed structure affects its meaning.
We plan extended texts with deliberate organisational choices, explaining how our structure serves our purpose.
